scheme_name: MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture
page_title: MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture

details: The State plan scheme “MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture” was launched by the Fisheries & Animal Resources Development Department, Government of Odisha under Mukhyamantri Maschyajibi Kalyan Yojana (MMKY) to promote farmers and entrepreneurs to take up Brackish water aquaculture.
To enhance brackish water shrimp and fish production in the state.
To enhance the livelihoods and income of coastal communities through the propagation of brackish water aquaculture.
To increase the shrimp/fish exports from the State.

The scheme shall be implemented in the coastal districts of Odisha as per the provisions of the Coastal Aquaculture Authority Act (CAA) - 2005 and Amendment Act- 2023, CAA Rules- 2005 and Amendment Rules-2022.
benefits: Excavation of New Brackish Water Ponds
' of 1.00 Ha. Under the State Sector Umbrella Scheme - MMKY:
Capital Cost for 1.00 Ha. Water Area
New tank of excavated area of 1.00 Ha. having 5 ft. depth
Input cost for one crop for 1.00 Ha. water area
Seed (Shrimp Post Larvae) @ 20 pieces per cubic meter
(The component-wise capital and input cost is only indicative. However, can be allowed as per actuals and considered on the basis of total operational cost rather than component-wise exact cost)
Subsidy shall be calculated and released to the beneficiary based on the actual cost incurred, subject to maximum subsidy as specified under the scheme.

Subsidy assistance @ 40% of the actual cost of implementation shall be provided to the General Category and 60% to Scheduled Castes (SC)/ Scheduled Tribes (ST)/Women/Transgender/ PwD Categories of beneficiaries. For PMMSY funds Transgender/PwD category shall not be considered.
In case the Government in F & ARD Department enhances the percentage of subsidy that shall be applicable for those beneficiaries who have received Go-ahead letters during F.Y 2023 24.
The scheme shall be implemented both under bank finance and self-finance.
Training and Skill Development of Beneficiaries:
To generate awareness, dissemination of farming technology and to attract the interested stakeholders on the benefit of the scheme, adequate training and awareness programs may be conducted.

The Directorate of Fisheries will either organise the training for beneficiaries as submitted by the DFOs at CIFA/BWTC/FTI etc. (Cost of the training shall be met out of the IEC/training and capacity building component, OR entrust the task to District/Zonal Level along with fund provision.
eligibility: The following are the eligibility criteria for the beneficiaries:
The beneficiary should be a resident of Odisha and must possess identity and residential proof including an Aadhaar Card.
The beneficiary should have a bank account in his/her name in the State of Odisha.
The beneficiary must have requisite land free from all encumbrances.
The beneficiary who has not availed assistance under this component shall be eligible for a subsidy maximum of up to 2.00 Ha water spread area.

The beneficiary who has availed similar subsidy earlier for excavation of Brackish water ponds under different schemes of Govt. of India or the State Government for less than 2.00 Ha. water spread area, he/she shall also be eligible to avail further subsidy for balance area keeping an overall maximum limit of up to 2.00 Ha. under the scheme.

While availing subsidy, the beneficiary family will be considered instead of individual members of the family. One beneficiary family should not be provided with multiple benefits under one particular scheme. A family will not be eligible for subsidy under the same scheme if any of its members have gotten a subsidy during the last three years.
Preference will be given for the selection of beneficiaries in cluster mode.

The documentary evidence in support of the suitable land needs to be provided by the beneficiary. (Copy of the RoR/Copy of the lease agreement in case of lease land for a minimum period of seven years of lease needs to be submitted).

offline: The applicant interested to avail of the subsidy shall apply to the Go-Sugam Portal through a registration process by submitting the requisite documents.
Click on “Apply” and then click on “Register Now”

On the registration page, fill in the mandatory details (Name, Email, Mobile Number, Address, and Password) and click on "Register". The login credentials will be sent to your registered Email ID / Mobile Number.
Click on “Scheme & Services” and then select the scheme by its name.

On the application page, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload the required documents in the appropriate file size and file type. Upon successful submission of the application, the applicant will get an Application Reference ID.
Click on the “Track Status” link on the menu.
Enter the Application ID or Mobile Number to get the application status.

documents_required: CAA Form-1 for registration of proposed land for brackish water aquaculture
Copy of the Lease Agreement (If required- a minimum period of seven years of lease land)
Affidavit for joint ownership ROR sworn before the Executive Magistrate

For differently-abled persons the certificate of disability issued by a certifying authority/competent authority indicating 40% or more disability as per 'The Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 will be acceptable.
The certificate of identity as transgender person issued by the District Magistrate as per "The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act 2019 will be accepted as Transgender category.

faqs.question: What is ‘MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture' Scheme?
faqs.answer: The 'MMKY-New Tank Excavation Under Brackish Water Aquaculture' scheme aims to encourage brackish water aquaculture among farmers and entrepreneurs in coastal districts of Odisha, under the Mukhyamantri Maschyajibi Kalyan Yojana (MMKY).

faqs.question: What are the main objectives of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The primary objectives of the scheme include: 1. Enhancing brackish water shrimp and fish production in the state. 2. Improving the livelihoods and income of coastal communities through the promotion of brackish water aquaculture. 3. Increasing shrimp/fish exports from the State.

faqs.question: Where is the scheme implemented?
faqs.answer: The scheme operates in the coastal districts of Odisha, following the provisions of the Coastal Aquaculture Authority Act (CAA) - 2005 and Amendment Act- 2023, CAA Rules- 2005, and Amendment Rules-2022.

faqs.question: Who can benefit from the MMKY-New Tank Excavation scheme?
faqs.answer: Farmers and entrepreneurs in the coastal districts of Odisha are the primary beneficiaries of the scheme.

faqs.question: How is the subsidy calculated for beneficiaries under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is calculated and released based on the actual cost incurred by the beneficiary, subject to the maximum subsidy as specified under the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the percentage of subsidy assistance provided to different beneficiary categories?
faqs.answer: Subsidy assistance is granted at a rate of 40% on the actual cost of implementation for the General Category beneficiaries. For Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Women, and Transgender categories, the subsidy assistance is higher at 60%. However, it's important to note that for PMMSY funds, the Transgender/PwD category shall not be considered for subsidy assistance.

faqs.question: How will the scheme be financed?
faqs.answer: The scheme will be implemented through a combination of bank finance and self-finance.

faqs.question: Why is training and skill development emphasized under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Training and skill development are essential components to generate awareness, disseminate farming technology, and attract stakeholders to the benefits of the scheme.

faqs.question: How will beneficiaries receive training under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Directorate of Fisheries may organize training programs at reputed institutions like CIFA/BWTC/FTI. Alternatively, training tasks may be entrusted to District/Zonal levels, along with fund provisions.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to participate in the scheme?
faqs.answer: To be eligible for the scheme, the beneficiary should be a resident of Odisha and must possess identity and residential proof, including an Aadhaar Card.

faqs.question: What land requirements are necessary for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The beneficiary must have requisite land free from all encumbrances to qualify for the scheme.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on the water spread area for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, a beneficiary who has not availed assistance under this component is eligible for a subsidy for a maximum of up to 2.00 Ha water spread area.

faqs.question: Can a beneficiary who has previously received a subsidy avail further benefits under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, a beneficiary who has availed a similar subsidy earlier for excavation of Brackish water ponds under different schemes of the Government of India or the State Government for less than 2.00 Ha water spread area is eligible to avail further subsidy for the balance area, with an overall maximum limit of up to 2.00 Ha under the scheme.

faqs.question: How is the eligibility of a family determined while availing subsidy?
faqs.answer: While availing subsidy, the beneficiary family will be considered instead of individual members of the family. One beneficiary family should not receive multiple benefits under one particular scheme. A family will not be eligible for subsidy under the same scheme if any of its members has received subsidy during the last three years.

faqs.question: What is the preference given for the selection of beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Preference will be given for the selection of beneficiaries in cluster mode.

faqs.question: How can an applicant apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Interested persons shall apply for registration of new brackish water farms through Go-Sugam portal. In case of difficulty in applying through Go-Sugam portal the hard copy of CAA Form No. I (Annexure-V) for fresh registration along with the Scheme application form (Annexure-I) to be submitted to the AFO for processing and digitalization of the proposal.
